title: Diplazium induratum
intro: Diplazium induratum, also known as the Indian shield fern, is a species of fern belonging to the family Athyriaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Diplazium induratum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Diplazium induratum is commonly prepared as a decoction or a powder, often in combination with other herbs, to create remedies for a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Diplazium induratum is classified within the family Athyriaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the lady fern or Marsh fern family. This family is part of the order Polypodiales, which includes a wide range of fern species. Diplazium induratum is further classified as a genus, Diplazium, which is a group of terrestrial ferns. The classification of Diplazium induratum into the family Athyriaceae reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other ferns. By understanding its classification, we can better comprehend the plant's ecological niche and its role in the ecosystem.
